source: Incus Server - 7 Container Configuration Options: incus info, resource limits, storage, and disk quotas

questions.q: Show detailed info (resources, profiles, IPs) for the container ubuntu-test.
questions.why: incus info <container> reports CPU/memory/disk/network usage and applied profiles.

questions.q: By default, how are a container's memory, disk, CPU assigned?
options: Incus assigns them automatically; you only set limits if you need to be specific
You must set every limit manually
Containers get no resources until configured
All resources are shared equally and fixed
questions.why: Incus manages resources well by default; explicit limits are optional and have trade-offs.

questions.q: What does `incus config set NAME limits.memory 2GB` do?
options: Sets a soft memory limit -- the container may exceed 2GB if memory is available
Sets a hard cap of exactly 2GB
questions.why: limits.memory is a soft limit; the container can use more when it is free.

options: Makes it a hard limit -- the container can never use more than 2GB
questions.why: limits.memory.enforce is the hard ceiling regardless of availability.

questions.q: What does `incus config set NAME limits.cpu 2` do?
options: Limits the container to 2 CPU cores
questions.why: limits.cpu restricts the number of CPU cores the container may use.

questions.q: On a ZFS pool, how do you set a 2GB disk quota on a container's root device?
options: incus config device override <name> root size=2GB
questions.why: config device override <name> root size=2GB caps the root disk (ZFS supports quotas).
